title: Restiaria madagascariensis
taxonomy: Restiaria madagascariensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae. This family is commonly known as the restio or rushes, and they are primarily found in southern Africa. Restionaceae is further classified as a family within the order Poales, which includes several other families of grasses and rushes. Poales is part of the monocot clade within the angiosperms, and it is further grouped under the larger clade called commelinids. The classification of Restiaria madagascariensis is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade angiosperms, clade monocots, order Poales, family Restionaceae, genus Restiaria.
